question: Where are swarm bifurcation points in thresholded policies?

hypothesis.H1: WIP threshold near 4-8 shows bifurcation in merge rate
hypothesis.H2: Mode enforcement ERROR (structural, L-791/S393) marks a step change vs advisory
hypothesis.H3: Two distinct bifurcation points identifiable from n=1000+ lanes

hypothesis_verdicts.H1: PARTIALLY WRONG — bifurcation is at WIP~20, not WIP 4-8. WIP 4-8 is a soft decline (13pp drop), not a step function. WIP 21+ = collapse (6.4% vs 85%+ below)
hypothesis_verdicts.H2: CONFIRMED — S393 is a hard bifurcation: 62.2%→100% step function at mode enforcement threshold
hypothesis_verdicts.H3: CONFIRMED — two types: (1) soft WIP gradient bifurcation at WIP~20, (2) hard policy bifurcation at S393

key_finding: Two bifurcation classes: (A) Soft/threshold: WIP 21+ triggers merge collapse (6.4%) vs gradual decline below. Fractal interpretation: WIP=20 is the coordination capacity limit. (B) Hard/policy: structural mode enforcement (S393) = qualitative step change in system behavior. Hard bifurcations are policy-created; soft bifurcations are capacity-emergent.
diff_from_prediction: Expected bifurcation at WIP=4-8 — actual is at WIP~20. The 4-8 zone shows linear decline, not a step. Mode enforcement hard bifurcation confirmed. SURPRISE: WIP=9 session has 100% merge rate (n=27) — suggests non-monotone local structure. The collapse is concentrated in WIP 21+, not a smooth boundary.

implications.F_FRA3_signal: F-FRA3 (fractal-complexity proxy) — two complexity regimes: WIP<=20 (manageable), WIP>20 (collapsed). The boundary is the WIP=20 threshold, not a continuous scaling function.
implications.F_STR3_signal: Campaign wave strategies should avoid sessions with WIP>20. Mode enforcement (S393) created a hard regime boundary — swarm now operates in a structurally different attractor.
